title: Implement message feed and cursor-based event stream
markdown:
## Goal

Implement a pull-based message feed with cursor tracking. External consumers (indexers, replicators, analytics) can request "give me all messages since cursor X" to process a reliable, ordered stream of changes.

## Background

The indexer needs to know which messages it has already processed. The feed provides a monotonic sequence of received messages with cursors. A cursor is an opaque string (timestamp-based or sequence-based) that marks a position in the stream.

The cursor store uses a B3nd node itself as storage: cursors are written to `mutable://indexer/{indexerName}/cursor`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `MessageFeedEntry` type: `{ cursor: string; uri: string; data: unknown; timestamp: number }`
- [ ] `MessageFeed` interface:
  - `append(uri: string, data: unknown): Promise<string>` -- append entry, return cursor
  - `read(options: { since?: string; limit?: number }): Promise<{ entries: MessageFeedEntry[]; nextCursor: string }>`
  - `latest(): Promise<string>` -- latest cursor
- [ ] `createMessageFeed(storage: NodeProtocolInterface)` factory that stores feed entries
- [ ] Feed entries stored at `mutable://feed/{timestamp}-{sequence}` URIs
- [ ] `CursorStore` class:
  - `get(indexerName: string): Promise<string | null>` -- get last processed cursor
  - `set(indexerName: string, cursor: string): Promise<void>` -- save cursor position
  - Uses a B3nd client to store at `mutable://indexer/{indexerName}/cursor`
- [ ] Feed processor: `createFeedProcessor(feed: MessageFeed)` that appends every received message to the feed
- [ ] Tests: append messages, read since cursor, read with limit, cursor store get/set, feed processor integration

## Implementation Guide

1. Create `libs/b3nd-indexer/feed.ts`:
   - `MessageFeedEntry` type
   - `MessageFeed` interface
   - In-memory implementation: sorted array of entries, cursor = `{timestamp}-{index}`
   - `read(since)`: binary search for cursor position, return slice
   - Persistence-backed implementation: stores entries as B3nd records
2. Create `libs/b3nd-indexer/cursor.ts`:
   - `CursorStore` that reads/writes cursor positions to a B3nd client
   - URI format: `mutable://indexer/{name}/cursor`
   - Data format: `{ cursor: string, updatedAt: number }`
3. Create `createFeedProcessor()` in `libs/b3nd-indexer/feed.ts`:
   - Returns a Processor that calls `feed.append(uri, data)` for every message
4. Create `libs/b3nd-indexer/feed_test.ts`
5. Export from `libs/b3nd-indexer/mod.ts`

## Files to Create/Modify

- `libs/b3nd-indexer/feed.ts` -- new: MessageFeed implementation and feed processor
- `libs/b3nd-indexer/cursor.ts` -- new: CursorStore implementation
- `libs/b3nd-indexer/feed_test.ts` -- new: tests
- `libs/b3nd-indexer/mod.ts` -- add exports

## Testing

```bash
deno test --allow-all libs/b3nd-indexer/feed_test.ts
```

## Dependencies

- **Depends on**: G1-A6-03 (processor pattern)
- **Blocks**: G1-A6-05 (backfill reads from feed / source node using cursors)
tasksExcerpt:
- [ ] Implement `IndexerDefinition` interface and runtime
- [ ] Implement `IndexerBackend` — at least one backend (SQLite or Postgres)
- [ ] Implement `createIndexerProcessor` — compose `Processor` that pipes messages to indexer
- [ ] Implement message feed / event stream (pull-based cursor, `GET /api/v1/feed?since={cursor}`)
- [ ] Implement backfill mechanism (paginate through source node, replay through indexer)
- [ ] Implement cursor store (using B3nd node itself as storage)
- [ ] Implement `
